zh.lifeSummary: 當Lord Richard Lee Mason of Minton於1376年出生在英國英格蘭史洛普爾邱奇 斯特頓時，他的父親Lord Richard Mason Of Chillwell26歲，母親Cathrine DeCharnes24歲。他於1401年與Lady Alice, Arundel De Burley在英國英格蘭史洛普爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1436年在他的家鄉享年60歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭史洛普爾邱奇 斯特頓聖 勞倫也斯 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lord Richard Lee Mason of Minton于1376年出生在英国英格兰史洛普爾邱奇 斯特頓时，他的父亲Lord Richard Mason Of Chillwell26岁，母亲Cathrine DeCharnes24岁。他于1401年与Lady Alice, Arundel De Burley在英国英格兰史洛普爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1436年在他的家乡享年60岁，埋葬在英国英格兰史洛普爾邱奇 斯特頓聖 勞倫也斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Lee
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sang, Jae, Jong, Jung, Sung, Yong, Kyung, Seung, Dong, Kwang, Myung.

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a meadow or a patch of arable land, Middle English lee, lea, from Old English lēa, dative case (used after a preposition) of lēah, which originally meant ‘wood or glade’.

English: habitational name from any of the many places in England named with Old English lēah ‘wood, glade’, including Lee in Buckinghamshire, Essex, Hampshire, Kent, and Shropshire, and Lea in Cheshire, Derbyshire, Herefordshire, Lancashire, Lincolnshire, and Wiltshire.

variants.name: Ley
meanings: English: variant of   Lee  .
German: from a medieval personal name, Eloy, from Latin Eligius (see Eligio ), a Christian saint's name.
German (Rhineland): topographic name from Middle High German leie ‘rock, stone, slate’, or a habitational name from any of several places called with this word. Compare Leier .

variants.name: Lees
meanings: English and Scottish: topographic name from Middle English lees ‘fields, arable land’, plural of lee (see Lee ), or from Middle English lese ‘pasture, meadow’ (Old English lǣs).

English: habitational name fromLeece or Lees in Lancashire, or Leese in Cheshire, all named from OldEnglish lēas ‘woodland clearings’ (plural oflēah), or from Leece in Cumbria, which was probably namedwith a Celtic word, lïss ‘hall, court, the principalhouse in a district’.
English: variant of   Leece   1.

variants.name: Lay
meanings: English: variant of   Lee  .
Scottish: shortened form of   McClay  .
German: habitational name from any of the places so named, in the Rhineland near Koblenz and in Bavaria, from lay(h), a word meaning ‘stone, rock, slate’.

variants.name: Laye
meanings: English: variant of   Lee  .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 李, based on its Cantonese pronunciation. See   Li   1.

variants.name: Lie
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Oystein, Sven, Thor.  German Hans, Gunther, Hilde.  Korean Han, Chang Ho, Chen, Chun, Hok, Jung, Kam, Liang, Lie, Ling, Shuen, Yung.
Norwegian: variant of Li 11, a habitational name from any of numerous farmsteads named Li, from Old Norse hlíth ‘mountain slope, hillside’. In Norway this is the most frequent spelling.
German: unexplained.

variants.name: Layman
meanings: English: topographic name for a dweller by a woodland clearing or a meadow, from Middle English ley + man. Compare Lee .
English: nickname from layman, a word denoting an uneducated person.
Americanized form of German   Lehmann  .

variants.name: Lea
meanings: English: variant of   Lee  .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 李 and 利, see   Li   1 and 3.

variants.name: Lye
meanings: English: variant of   Lee   1, a topographic name for someone who lived near a patch of arable land, or a habitational name from Lye in Herefordshire (with the same etymology).

Norwegian: habitational name from a farmstead in Rogaland named Lye, Old Norse Lýgi meaning ‘alliance, covenant’, used to denote a place sanctified by such an agreement, such as a court or council meeting place.
Altered form of German   Ley  .

variants.name: Saelee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Chan, Kao, Nai, Chai, Cheng, Chien, Foo, Hin, Muang, Pao, Seng, Song, Sou.
Southeast Asian (Iu Mien): from the name of the Lee clan of the Iu Mien people from Laos + the Thai prefix sae, denoting a clan. Compare Lee 8.

variants.name: Leigh
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places (in at least sixteen counties, but especially Leigh in Lancashire) named either with the nominative case of Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’ (see Lee ) or with lēage, a late dative form of this word (see Lye ).
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 李, based on its Cantonese pronunciation. See   Li   1.

nameMeanings.name: Mason
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English masoun ‘mason, stone worker, builder in stone’ (Old Central French maçon, masson). A Middle English form machun (derived from Old French machun) gives rise to Machin and its variants. See also Mayson . Stonemasonry was a hugely important craft in the Middle Ages.
Italian (Veneto): variant of   Masone  .
Altered form of French   Masson  .

variants.name: Masson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Lucien, Alain, Henri, Pierre, Yves, Agathe, Aime, Cecile, Clovis, Francois, Francoise.
Scottish: variant of   Mason   ‘mason’.
Scottish: variant of   Meston  .

variants.name: Machin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Juan, Jose, Carlos, Jorge, Mario, Miguel, Pedro, Blanca, Diego, Luis, Ramon.
English: variant of   Machen  . This is the more common form of the name in Britain and Ireland.
Spanish (Machín): probably a nickname from machín ‘boor, lout’, often applied to a blacksmith's apprentice.

variants.name: Macon
meanings: French: habitational name from any of several places so called, e.g. in Saône-et-Loire.
French: from the old oblique form of the ancient Germanic personal name Mako, a variant of Mago, a short form of compound names formed with maht ‘strength, power’ as the first element.
French (Maçon): occupational name for a mason, (Old) French maçon.

variants.name: Machen
meanings: English: variant of Mason , from the Middle English form machun (derived from Old French machun).

variants.name: Meason
meanings: English: variant of   Mason  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rickert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Lord Richard Lee Mason of Minton was born in 1376, in Church Stretton, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Lord Richard Mason Of Chillwell, was 26 and his mother, Cathrine DeCharnes, was 24. He married Lady Alice, Arundel De Burley in 1401, in Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1436, in his hometown, at the age of 60, and was buried in St Laurence's Church, Church Stretton, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Lady Alice, Arundel De Burley en 1401, à Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. it.lifeSummary: Quando Lord Richard Lee Mason of Minton è nato il nel 1376 nel Church Stretton, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Lord Richard Mason Of Chillwell, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Cathrine DeCharnes, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Lady Alice, Arundel De Burley nel 1401, nel Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. ja.lifeSummary: Lord Richard Lee Mason of Mintonが1376年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアチャーチ ストレットオンで生まれた時，父Lord Richard Mason Of Chillwellは26歳，母Cathrine DeCharnesは24歳であった。1401年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアでLady Alice, Arundel De Burleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1436年に故郷で60歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアチャーチ ストレットオンスト ラウレンセ'ス チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.Saint Laurence's Church, Church Stretton: 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 c후르치 스트레턴 스트 로렌스'스 c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: Lord Richard Lee Mason of Minton(이)가 1376년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 c후르치 스트레턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Lord Richard Mason Of Chillwell(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Cathrine DeCharnes(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1401년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르에서 Lady Alice, Arundel De Burley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1436년 그의 고향에서 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 c후르치 스트레턴 스트 로렌스'스 c후르치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lord Richard Lee Mason of Minton nasceu em 1376, em Church Stretton, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Lord Richard Mason Of Chillwell, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Cathrine DeCharnes, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Alice, Arundel De Burley em 1401, em Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1436, em sua cidade natal, com 60 anos, e foi sepultado em St Laurence's Church, Church Stretton, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Saint Laurence's Church, Church Stretton: ст лоуренсе'с чурч, чурч стреттон, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lord Richard Lee Mason of Minton родился в 1376 г. в местности чурч стреттон, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Lord Richard Mason Of Chillwell было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Cathrine DeCharnes, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Alice, Arundel De Burley в 1401 г. в местности шропшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1436 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 60 лет и был похоронен в местности ст лоуренсе'с чурч, чурч стреттон, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lord Richard Lee Mason of Minton nació en 1376, en Church Stretton, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Lord Richard Mason Of Chillwell, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Cathrine DeCharnes, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Lady Alice, Arundel De Burley en 1401, en Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1436, en su pueblo, a la edad de 60 años, y fue sepultado en St Laurence's Church, Church Stretton, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
